title: Maximum Number of Distinct Elements After Operations
content:
You are given an integer array nums and an integer k.

You are allowed to perform the following operation on each element of the array at most once:

• Add an integer in the range [-k, k] to the element.

Return the maximum possible number of distinct elements in nums after performing the operations.

Example 1:

Input: nums = [1,2,2,3,3,4], k = 2

Output: 6

Explanation: nums changes to [-1, 0, 1, 2, 3, 4] after performing operations on the first four elements.

Example 2:

Input: nums = [4,4,4,4], k = 1

Output: 3

Explanation: By adding -1 to nums[0] and 1 to nums[1], nums changes to [3, 5, 4, 4].

Constraints:

• 1 <= nums.length <= 105

• 1 <= nums[i] <= 109

• 0 <= k <= 109

        :rtype: int

hints: Find the minimum element which is not used for each element.

solutions.explanation:
Sort nums. Process in sorted order; for each element, greedily assign the smallest available value in [v-k, v+k] not yet used. Since nums is sorted and we always try the smallest candidate, each candidate only increases monotonically — the while loop is O(n) total across all elements.

Time: O(n log n + n). Space: O(n).
